intro: Taraktogenos merrilliana, also known as Philippine taro or mountain taro, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Taraktogenos merrilliana include taraxasterol, taraxeryl acetate, and taraxerol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make various herbal preparations, such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin infections.
